data.title: IBM raises dividend by 0.6% to $1.69
data.description:

data.title: Medpace GAAP EPS of $4.28 beats by $0.40, revenue of $706.6M beats by $8.97M
data.description:
• Medpace press release (MEDP): Q1 GAAP EPS of $4.28 beats by $0.40.
  • Revenue of $706.6M (+26.5% Y/Y) beats by $8.97M.
  • Revenue of $706.6 million in the first quarter of 2026 increased 26.5% from revenue of $558.6 million for the comparable prior-year period, representing a backlog conversion rate of 23.3%.
  • Net new business awards were $618.4 million in the first quarter of 2026, representing an increase of 23.7% from net new business awards of $500.0 million for the comparable prior-year period, which resulted in a net book-to-bill ratio of 0.88x.
  • Backlog as of March 31, 2026 increased 2.9% to $2,929.2 million from $2,846.0 million as of March 31, 2025. Net new business awards were $618.4 million, representing a net book-to-bill ratio of 0.88x for the first quarter of 2026, as compared to $500.0 million for the comparable prior-year period. The Company calculates the net book-to-bill ratio by dividing net new business awards by revenue.

data.title: Stocks to watch on Wednesday after hours: TSLA, LRCX, TXN
data.description:
Wall Street’s major averages finished higher on Wednesday, buoyed by easing geopolitical tensions after Donald Trump announced an extension to the ceasefire deadline with Iran. The move, coupled with his remarks highlighting internal divisions within Tehran’s leadership, helped lift investor sentiment and supported a broader risk-on tone across equities.

Now here are the 3 stocks to watch on Wednesday after hours:

Tesla (TSLA) shares climbed 3.7% in after-hours trading after the EV maker delivered a solid earnings beat, reporting on-GAAP EPS of $0.41, topping expectations by $0.06. Revenue came in at $22.39B, ahead of estimates by $190M, reinforcing resilience in demand and supporting a positive initial market reaction.

Lam Research (LRCX) shares jumped 4.3% in after-hours trading following a strong quarterly print, with non-GAAP EPS of $1.47 coming in $0.11 above expectations. Revenue totaled $5.84B, topping estimates by $80M, as solid demand trends and execution across its core segments helped drive an upbeat initial reaction from investors.

Texas Instruments (TXN) surged 7.9% in after-hours trading after delivering a standout quarterly report, with GAAP EPS of $1.68 exceeding expectations by $0.32. Revenue came in at $4.83B, beating estimates by $300M, as strength across its analog and embedded segments helped fuel a sharp positive reaction from investors.

data.title: United outlines $7-$11 full-year 2026 EPS range as it targets 85%-100% fuel pass-through by Q4
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: United Airlines (UAL) Q1 2026

Management View

  • "I'd like to congratulate the United team on a strong first quarter." (CEO & Director Scott Kirby) "More immediately, of course, we're managing through the impact of jet fuel prices that have doubled."
  • "At the moment, our goal is to do whatever it takes to recover 100% of the increase in jet fuel prices as quickly as possible and to achieve double-digit pretax margins next year." (CEO & Director Kirby) "For United, that means we're targeting capacity to be flat to up 2% for 3Q and 4Q on a year-over-year basis."
  • "During the first quarter of 2026, United carried a record number of passengers while also navigating a challenging operating environment." (President Brett Hart) "Late last week, the FAA issued an order regarding the summer 2026 schedule at Chicago O'Hare. We are currently reviewing the FAA order and we'll share additional information, including any next steps as soon as our review is complete."
  • "We are pleased to reach a tentative agreement during the quarter with our flight attendants represented by the Association of Flight Attendants." (President Hart) "Voting concludes on May 12."
  • "Consolidated total operating revenue in Q1 increased 10.6% year-over-year to a record first quarter of $14.6 billion." (Executive VP & Chief Commercial Officer Andrew Nocella) "Late in the first quarter, we implemented 5 broadly successful price increases, along with an increase in baggage fees that began to offset the increase in the price of jet fuel."
  • "We have now formally announced these initiatives" including "nested selling" and "base fares in our premium cabins" as well as "50 A321 Coastliners" and "United's new Airbus A321 XLR onboard products." (Executive VP Nocella) "Combined between the Coastliner and the XLR, we expect to have a fleet of 100 A321s equipped with 20 lay flat beds and 12 premium plus seats."
  • "We delivered resilient results with first quarter earnings per share of $1.19" and "our pretax margin was 3.4%." (Executive VP & CFO Michael Leskinen) "Looking ahead, we expect second quarter EPS to be between $1 and $2, anchored by an all-in fuel average price of approximately $4.30 per gallon."

Outlook

  • "We expect to deliver full year 2026 EPS in the $7 to $11 range." (Executive VP Leskinen) "In both higher and lower fuel price scenarios, we expect to recapture 40% to 50% of the increased fuel cost in the second quarter, 70% to 80% in the third quarter and 85% to 100% by the fourth quarter."
  • "We now expect Q3 and Q4 capacity to be flat to up approximately 2%." (Executive VP Nocella) "In response to higher fuel cost environment, we've begun to adjust capacity downward by approximately 5 points throughout the rest of the year."
  • Compared with the prior quarter’s EPS framework, "for the full year 2026, we expect earnings per share to be between $12 and $14" was replaced by "full year 2026 EPS in the $7 to $11 range" after management said "jet fuel prices" have "doubled." (Executive VP Leskinen; CEO & Director Kirby)

Financial Results

  • "Consolidated total operating revenue in Q1 increased 10.6% year-over-year to a record first quarter of $14.6 billion." (Executive VP Nocella) "TRASM increased by 6.9% year-over-year."
  • "We delivered resilient results with first quarter earnings per share of $1.19" despite "a $340 million higher fuel bill in the quarter." (Executive VP Leskinen) "Our CASM-ex for the first quarter was up 5.9% year-over-year."
  • "In the quarter, we took actions" including paying down "more than $3.1 billion in debt" and "in the first quarter, we generated $2.9 billion in free cash flow." (Executive VP Leskinen) "the first quarter marked United's return to the unsecured market as we raised $2 billion across two unsecured bonds."

Q&A

  • Jamie Baker, JPMorgan Chase & Co: Asked about a potential Europe hub and the role of alliances; CEO & Director Kirby responded, "I think it's extremely unlikely that we'll open a foreign hub" and added, "Our Star Alliance partnerships are great. They enable global reach and breadth."
  • Jamie Baker, JPMorgan Chase & Co: Asked about a reported Spirit rescue; CEO & Director Kirby said, "I hope that doesn't happen" and added, "whether Spirit fails or keeps flying, I don't think it has much effect on United one way or another."
  • Conor Cunningham, Melius Research: Pressed on demand destruction in a prolonged high-fuel scenario; Executive VP Nocella said, "we've begun to adjust capacity downward" and "demand is hanging in there really strong," while Executive VP Leskinen added, "there's nothing in our bookings that suggests there's demand destruction... But I believe it's prudent to be prepared for that."
  • Ravi Shanker, Morgan Stanley: Asked about fuel availability risk; Executive VP Leskinen said, "We've got really good visibility for 4 or 5 weeks" and "as we sit here today, we think it is a price issue, not an availability issue."
  • Scott Group, Wolfe Research: Asked why fare increases require a crisis and whether higher yields can stick; CEO & Director Kirby said, "airfares in real terms are down 27%, 2025 versus pre-pandemic" and added, "if things went back to mid-February normal, I think we get -- keep 20% of the price increase next year."
  • Brandon Oglenski, Barclays: Asked about Chicago O’Hare and the FAA cap; CEO & Director Kirby said, "we've actually won 38 points of market share with business travelers" and added, "it does look like the FAA is going to not let us grow as much as we and our customers would have liked."
  • Andrew Didora, BofA Securities: Asked about maintenance/CASM-ex and capacity cuts; Executive VP Leskinen said, "you should broadly expect our CASM-ex trends to move inversely with the amount of capacity that we take out" and added, "I'm very optimistic we will not face that same trend that much of the industry faces."
  • Duane Pfennigwerth, Evercore ISI: Asked about credit-card uptake and timing for a new co-brand agreement; Executive VP Nocella said, "the uplift, the spend has been incredible" and added, "I think you can Google the expiration date and know that it's not tomorrow, but it's not that far off."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was neutral-to-slightly negative, concentrating on fuel pass-through durability, potential demand destruction, potential fuel shortages, regulatory constraints at Chicago O’Hare, and competitive/industry actions (including Spirit), reflected in questions on "fuel availability" (Ravi Shanker, Morgan Stanley) and the mechanics of holding higher pricing (Christian Wetherbee, Wells Fargo).
  • Management’s tone was confident in pricing power but cautious on macro volatility, with repeated emphasis on preparation and scenario planning: "we remain confident in our ability to fully recapture the fuel cost increases over time" (Executive VP Nocella) and "we are managing the business with the expectation that jet fuel remains elevated" (Executive VP Leskinen).
  • Versus the previous quarter’s more upbeat setup ("2026 sets up as more of the same at United, but with a much better industry backdrop" (CEO & Director Kirby, Q4)), the current call repeatedly framed an active shock response ("jet fuel prices that have doubled" (CEO & Director Kirby)) and widened ranges.

Quarter-over-quarter Comparison

  • Guidance posture shifted from "for the full year 2026, we expect earnings per share to be between $12 and $14" (Executive VP Leskinen, Q4) to "full year 2026 EPS in the $7 to $11 range" alongside fuel-driven scenario planning. (Executive VP Leskinen, Q1)
  • Capacity strategy language moved from concluding the United Next connectivity push ("2025 represents United's high watermark on domestic capacity growth" (Executive VP Nocella, Q4)) to explicit near-term cuts ("proactively removing about 5 points of capacity" and "Q3 and Q4 capacity to be flat to up approximately 2%" (Executive VP Nocella, Q1)).
  • Commercial roadmap evolved from previewing merchandising changes ("the largest redesign of united.com in a decade" planned (Executive VP Nocella, Q4)) to announcing and activating initiatives ("nested selling... is now active" and "we have already seen large increases in upselling" (Executive VP Nocella, Q1)).

Risks and Concerns

  • "Fuel prices remain volatile" and management cited the "massive run-up in fuel prices created by the conflict in Iran" as the central shock. (Executive VP Leskinen)
  • Operational and network risks included "elevated weather events and geopolitical disruptions" (President Hart) and "pulling our Tel Aviv and Dubai flights, which together were 1.5 points of our capacity." (Executive VP Leskinen)
  • Regulatory uncertainty centered on Chicago O’Hare: "the FAA issued an order regarding the summer 2026 schedule at Chicago O'Hare" with the company "currently reviewing the FAA order." (President Hart)

Final Takeaway

United management described a Q1 shaped by a rapid fuel shock, emphasizing price actions, baggage-fee increases, and a planned capacity pullback designed to accelerate fuel-cost recapture through 2026, while maintaining a longer-term target of double-digit pretax margins. Executives highlighted newly announced merchandising, product, and fleet initiatives—especially expanded premium narrowbody deployments—and paired the updated earnings framework with balance-sheet actions that included substantial debt paydown, a return to unsecured issuance, and sizable free cash flow generation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: ASGN Non-GAAP EPS of $0.69 misses by $0.29, revenue of $968.3M misses by $4.02M
data.description:
• ASGN press release (ASGN): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.69 misses by $0.29.
  • Revenue of $968.3M (flat Y/Y) misses by $4.02M.
  • Consolidated revenues for the quarter were $968.3 million, flat compared with the first quarter of 2025. Commercial Segment revenues were 70 percent of total revenues and were $675.5 million, up from $672.2 million in the first quarter of 2025. Federal Government Segment revenues were 30 percent of total revenues and were $292.8 million, compared with $296.1 million in the prior-year period.
  • Shares -20%.

data.title: Target shares fall after six-session winning streak
data.description:
Target (TGT) shares snapped six straight sessions of gains on Wednesday, as the stock was 1.1% lower at $130.71.

The general merchandise retailer gained over 10% in the preceding six sessions. So far this year, the stock has added 34%, compared to the 3% drop in the broader S&P 500 Index (SP500).

TGT is up 14% over the past one month.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, TGT has a Hold rating with a score of 3.4 out of 5. The company received A in the prospect of profitability, while it received F in the growth factor.

Seeking Alpha analysts are cautious and see the stock as a Hold.

Turning to the Wall Street, 11 analysts have given the stock a Buy or above rating. 23 gave the stock hold recommendation, while three gave Sell rating to TGT.

 

“Target Corporation remains resilient through prudent cost management, diversified product lines, and robust liquidity but faces persistent macroeconomic headwinds,” pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

data.title: TSMC debuts new A13, N2U chip manufacturing ‌technology
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSM) on Wednesday launched its newest generation of chip manufacturing ‌technology aimed at creating smaller and faster chips, especially for artificial intelligence.

TSMC, which makes chips for some of the world's largest tech companies including Nvidia (NVDA) Apple (AAPL) and Google (GOOG) (GOOGL), debuted two latest innovations in its chipmaking technology. One is the new A13 process, which is a direct shrink of its A14 node announced in 2025 and most likely will be useful for artificial intelligence chips, and another one called N2U - a more affordable option to make chips for mobile applications, laptops, and AI.

The company said A13 is scheduled to enter production in 2029, while N2U is set for production in 2028.

TSMC is also planning to squeeze more gains out of its existing extreme-ultraviolet lithography (EUV) ​machines from Dutch supplier ASML (ASML), rather than move to a newer generation of "high NA" EUV machines, which, at $400 million each, are ​roughly double the cost of the older machines, reported Reuters.

TSMC is ASML’s largest customer, according to Bloomberg’s supply chain data. According to Bloomberg, the company will hold off on deploying ASML's most cutting-edge lithography machines for chip production through 2029 to save money.

Shares of ASML fell over 1%, while shares of TSMC rose 5% in the afternoon.

During TSMC’s 2026 North America Technology Symposium, the company also showed plans for new technologies to support AI demand for more computing power and memory in a single package.

TSMC said it continues to expand its Chip on Wafer on Substrate (CoWoS) technology to integrate more silicon and is now producing 5.5-reticle size CoWoS and planning for even larger versions. The company is also offering its TSMC-SoIC 3D chip stacking technology on its most advanced technology platform, with A14-to-A14 SoIC set to be available for production in 2029.

“At TSMC, we understand our customers are always looking ahead to their next innovation and they come to us for a reliable stream of new silicon technologies, like A13, meticulously engineered to be ready for high-volume production right when their visionary new designs demand them,” said TSMC chief executive C.C. Wei. 

Earlier in the month, TSMC raised its revenue outlook for 2026, driven by AI chip demand, after the global foundry's first quarter net income surged 58% year-over-year.

The global foundry’s stock has gained over 22% so far this year, outperforming the 3% rise in the broader S&P 500 index.
